title: Ignore Rules, Attributes, Line Endings, and Repository Content · Spiral Review
summary: Keep generated output, local configuration, binaries, and secrets out of history while preserving cross-platform text behavior. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a content-boundary and normalization policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace ignore sources, directory scope, pattern grammar, negation, tracked-state limits, and check-ignore evidence.
Choose repository ignore, info/exclude, and global excludes from whether a rule belongs to every collaborator or one environment.
Use attributes for text normalization, diff, merge, export, and binary behavior with check-attr evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — Repository tracks config.local, build/app.bin, and CRLF script check.sh; .gitignore is added afterward. Decide tracked cleanup, attribute normalization, secret rotation, and changed-platform evidence.

Inspect the Secret Large Generated Data trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for prevent and respond to secrets, large binaries, generated artifacts, and sensitive history with policy and rotation boundaries.

content.text: For Secret Large Generated Data, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Ignore Precedence evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Ignore Precedence assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Repository tracks config.local, build/app.bin, and CRLF script check.sh; .gitignore is added afterward. Decide tracked cleanup, attribute normalization, secret rotation, and changed-platform evidence.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph

Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Secret Large Generated Data: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Repository tracks config.local, build/app.bin, and CRLF script check.sh; .gitignore is added afterward. Decide tracked cleanup, attribute normalization, secret rotation, and changed-platform evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prevent and respond to secrets, large binaries, generated artifacts, and sensitive history with policy and rotation boundaries.
